title: pinus cembroides subsp. edulis
intro: Pinus cembroides subsp. edulis, commonly known as the Mexican piñon or pinyon pine, is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are believed to aid in reducing pain and fever. The health benefits of Pinus cembroides subsp. edulis include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant, helping to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The seeds can be prepared into various herbal products, such as tea, oil, and flour, which are often used in traditional medicine and as a food source.
